_date_from_comment: ISO 8601 string ('2026-01-01' or '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z'). Null = no lower bound. Used by STEP 4.1.
_date_to_comment: ISO 8601 string. Null = no upper bound. Used by STEP 4.1.
_runtime_paths_comment: Optional path overrides are defined in config_runtime.json.
_z_position_config_comment: Set to [z1, z2, z3, z4] to select a specific z config, e.g. [0.0, 100.0, 200.0, 400.0]. If null, a random one is chosen.

step_1_1._path_overrides_comment: Optional simulation_params_csv override is defined at top-level in config_runtime.json.

step_1_2._eff_fit_polynomial_order_comment: Polynomial order n for empirical-vs-simulated fits used in STEP 1.2 and STEP 4.2 efficiency correction.
step_1_2._plot_parameters_comment: Parameters to include in scatter matrix, histograms, and coverage plots. Use any subset of param_cols.

step_1_3._enabled_comment: Single switch for STEP 1.3 enlargement. If enabled and enlarged dataset exists, STEP 2 uses it automatically; otherwise STEP 2 falls back to STEP 1.2 dataset.
step_1_3._n_new_rows_comment: Exact number of synthetic rows to add. If null, use size_multiplier.
step_1_3._size_multiplier_comment: When n_new_rows is null: added_rows = round((size_multiplier - 1) * N_base).
step_1_3._random_seed_comment: Null = random seed (stored in summary JSON). Set integer for deterministic enlargement.
step_1_3._events_per_file_comment: If set, use constant n_events for all new points. If null and sample_n_events_from_base=true, n_events are sampled from base dataset.
step_1_3._sample_n_events_from_base_comment: When events_per_file is null, sample n_events from the base dataset distribution to keep realistic event-count spread.
step_1_3._uniform_jitter_fraction_comment: Uniform target generation uses one point per randomized grid cell in (flux,eff). 0=center of each cell, 1=full-cell jitter.
step_1_3._flux_eff_range_comment: Null = full dictionary bounds for chosen flux/eff columns. If set, range is clamped to dictionary bounds.
step_1_3._target_generation_comment: STEP 1.3 now enlarges with uniform-density points in (flux,eff), not a STEP 3.1 curve trajectory.
step_1_3._weighting_shared_comment: Centralized shared weighting controls. STEP 1.3 and STEP 3.2 both read this block; null entries fall back to internal code defaults.
step_1_3._weighting_overrides_comment: Optional final overrides on top of step_1_3.weighting_shared (kept for backward compatibility).

step_2_1._feature_columns_comment: List of column names to use as feature vector, or 'auto' to use the most advanced available *_tt_*_rate_hz prefix (excluding single-plane *_tt_[0-4]_rate_hz columns).
step_2_1._plot_parameters_comment: Shared STEP 2 parameter selection: controls STEP 2.1 true-vs-estimated panels, the STEP 2.1 random-showcase matrix axes, and STEP 2.2 validation plots.
step_2_1._interpolation_k_comment: If null, use all dictionary entries for interpolation. Otherwise, use k-nearest neighbors in feature space.
step_2_1._showcase_seed_comment: Seed for random showcase point selection. Null/'auto'/'random' = different point each run; integer = reproducible point.
step_2_1._showcase_fixed_tolerance_pct_comment: Global tolerance (%) used when fixing non-active parameters in showcase slices. For each fixed parameter, choose the closest available value within +/- tolerance_pct * parameter_span / 100, where parameter_span=max(candidate)-min(candidate). If none are within band, that subplot is left as N/A.

step_2_2._relerr_threshold_pct_comment: Maximum relative error (%) for robust color scaling in contour plots.
step_2_2._relerr_plot_limits_pct_comment: Relative-error plotting window [min, max] in percent, used in validation and error-vs-events plots.

step_2_3._n_bins_per_param_comment: Set an integer to use the same bins for all parameters, or a dict with per-parameter bin counts.
step_2_3._exclude_dictionary_entries_comment: If true, rows marked true_is_dictionary_entry are removed before LUT/error calculations.
step_2_3._relerr_filter_pct_comment: Signed relative-error window [min, max] (%) applied before histogramming and LUT statistics.
step_2_3._outlier_iqr_factor_comment: Outliers are defined as points outside [Q1 - factor*IQR, Q3 + factor*IQR].

step_3_1._path_overrides_comment: Optional source_csv/rate_dictionary_csv overrides are defined in config_runtime.json.
step_3_1._source_table_comment: dataset or dictionary. Used when source_csv is null.
step_3_1._flux_range_comment: Set [min, max] for flux movement. Use equal values for constant flux.
step_3_1._eff_range_comment: Set [min, max] for efficiency movement. Use equal values for constant efficiency.
step_3_1._dense_points_comment: High-resolution points used for event integration along the smooth trajectory.
step_3_1._random_seed_comment: (1784447138, 1168061690 was nice) - Set an integer to reproduce exactly the same curve. Null means random seed each run (saved in summary JSON).
step_3_1._events_per_file_comment: Target counts per synthetic file used to discretize the trajectory.
step_3_1._include_partial_last_file_comment: If true, include final partial segment when remaining events are below events_per_file.
step_3_1._n_harmonics_comment: Higher values add more local structure while staying smooth.
step_3_1._roughness_comment: Higher values suppress high-frequency components (smoother look).

step_3_2._path_overrides_comment: Optional CSV path overrides moved to config_runtime.json.
step_3_2._weighting_source_comment: All STEP 3.2 weighting settings are defined once in step_1_3.weighting_shared.

step_4_1._station_id_comment: Station selector for STEP 4 real-data collection. Use 1 or 'MINGO01'. Null falls back to top-level station_id.
step_4_1._task_ids_comment: Task IDs for STEP 4 real-data collection (e.g. [1] or [1,2,3,4,5]). Null falls back to top-level task_ids.
step_4_1._path_overrides_comment: Optional dictionary_csv path override moved to config_runtime.json.
step_4_1._global_rate_col_comment: Preferred global-rate column name (Hz) for STEP 4.1/4.2 diagnostics.
step_4_1._timestamp_column_comment: Column used for latest-execution aggregation per filename_base.
step_4_1._date_from_comment: Optional ISO 8601 lower bound applied to data-time parsed from filename_base (inclusive).
step_4_1._date_to_comment: Optional ISO 8601 upper bound applied to data-time parsed from filename_base (inclusive).
step_4_1._timeline_freq_comment: Pandas time-floor frequency used for timeline coverage plot bins (e.g., D, 12H, W).

step_4_2._criteria_source_comment: STEP 4.2 matching criteria are always inherited from step_2_1 (feature_columns, distance_metric, interpolation_k, include_global_rate, global_rate_col).
step_4_2._eff2_global_rate_eff_source_comment: Controls y-efficiency source in STEP_4_2_5 plot: auto | transformed | raw. Auto falls back to raw when transformed dictionary eff2 is largely outside [0,1].
step_4_2._n_events_column_comment: Column used as n_events for LUT interpolation. 'auto' selects the best available count-like column.
step_4_2._coverage_frequency_comment: Time-bin frequency for coverage-fraction plot when timestamps are available.
